title: crepis modocensis subsp. rostrata
intro: Crepis modocensis subsp. rostrata, also known as rostrate hawksbeard, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family and is native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, while its herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and topical creams that can be applied directly to affected areas. Additionally, the rostrate hawksbeard plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, coughs, and sore throats.
